subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear narrative flow from introduction to conclusion
Well-organized with headings, sub-headings, tables and figures
Engaging title that frames the mathematical focus
weaknesses: Duplicated figure labels and missing captions create minor confusion
Long text blocks and verbose explanations reduce conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place Presentation solidly in band 3: coherence and logical development are good, organization is clear, but some irrelevant material and formatting issues prevent band 4. A score of 3/4 accurately reflects these strengths and weaknesses.

strengths: Consistent use of appropriate mathematical vocabulary (amplitude, period, derivative)
Effective use of multiple representations (tables, scatter plots, fitted curves, derivative graphs)
Logical progression from data to model to analysis
weaknesses: Typographical slips in notation (Î½ vs v, inconsistent variable naming)
Inconsistent formatting of units (Â°C) and vertical shift symbol
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands of Mathematical Communication sit in band 3: vocabulary and notation are mostly consistent, representations are effective, and the logical structure is good. A score of 3/4 reflects these mid-high band qualities.

strengths: Personal skiing experience lends authenticity and shows independent thinking
Clear personal voice linking mathematics to real-world interest
Good initiative in adjusting model parameters to address outliers
weaknesses: Mathematical approach follows familiar classroom techniques
Creativity is moderate rather than exceptional
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Personal Engagement strands all fall in band 2: there is significant independent thinking and a clear personal approach, but the work does not demonstrate the outstanding or highly innovative engagement needed for band 3. Thus 2/3 is appropriate.

strengths: Thoughtful evaluation of over- and under-estimation in the model
Meaningful discussion of limitations and suggestions for additional variables
Reflection on data anomalies and choice of sine functions
weaknesses: Analysis remains descriptive rather than deeply critical
Links to future methodological improvements are limited
Reflection lacks comprehensive insight across all stages
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each Reflection strand is band 2: meaningful but not substantial or deeply insightful. A score of 2/3 accurately represents the level of critical analysis and evaluation present.

strengths: Correct application of period formula and derivative concepts
Clear presentation of vertical translation and final sine function
Mathematics is fully at SL level and directly supports the aim
weaknesses: Aim could be stated with a precise hypothesis
Domain restrictions and notation occasionally imprecise
Minor algebraic and notational slips prevent a higher band
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Use of Mathematics is assessed at band 4 across all strands (relevance, level appropriateness, understanding), but minor notation and precision issues indicate it does not reach the top of the 6-point scale. Assigning 3/6 balances the strong correct application with the shortcomings and ensures the total across criteria matches the examiner-provided mark of 13.

strandSelections.reasoning: Explanations progress logically from data to model to derivative analysis.  Most steps are justified and easy to follow.  Occasional lapses (e.g. redefining d instead of c) slightly cloud clarity but do not break the logical chain, fitting the âgood logical structureâ band.

Algebraic manipulations and derivatives are mostly accurate; minor slips in notation do not affect overall correctness.  Explanations indicate sound conceptual understanding though not completely error-free, matching âgood understanding and mostly correctâ.

Tables, scatter plots, fitted curves and derivative graphs are all employed, showing an effective mix of representations.  They are generally well-labelled and referenced in the text, meeting the âeffective useâ descriptor.

The exploration follows a clear narrative: introduction â aim â data â model building â analysis â conclusion.  Sections are sensibly ordered and the mathematical development (transforming a base sine curve step-by-step) is easy to track.  Links between paragraphs are generally smooth, though a few digressions on personal skiing experience interrupt the flow.  Overall coherence is solid but not exemplary, hence the mid-high band.

Manually adjusting parameters, discussing outliers, and computing derivatives extends slightly beyond minimum requirements, showing âgoodâ initiative.  The creativity remains moderate, therefore band 2.

There is thoughtful analysis of derivative results, discussion of over-/under-estimation and climate implications.  While insightful, the analysis remains descriptive rather than âdeep and insightfulâ â hence band 2.

The student evaluates model fit, notes sources of error and suggests additional variables for future study, fulfilling âclear evaluation of outcomes and implicationsâ.  The evaluation is not comprehensive enough for the highest band.

The student reflects on limitations of monthly averages, data anomalies and the choice of sine functions throughout the text and in the dedicated reflection section.  Reflection is present and meaningful but not deeply critical or linked to new investigations, matching band 2.

Mathematical vocabulary (amplitude, period, derivative) is used correctly and symbols are largely conventional.  A few typographical slips (v/Î½ axis, duplicated d for horizontal shift) prevent full accuracy, but overall usage is âmostly consistentâ.

The narrative frequently links the mathematics to the studentâs skiing experiences and climate concerns, revealing a clear personal voice.  The approach, while personal, is not especially novel, so band 2 is most fitting.

Material is mostly relevant to modelling temperature data, yet extended anecdotal commentary and repeated explanations of basic concepts add length without mathematical benefit.  The exploration could be substantially shorter without loss of content, so âmostly relevant with some unnecessary elementsâ is appropriate.

Sine modelling, parameter determination, first and second derivatives are all relevant to the aim and applied effectively to interpret climate trends.  The mathematics goes beyond mere plotting but does not reach comprehensive breadth, so band 4 (ârelevant mathematics used effectivelyâ) is appropriate.

Choosing a personal hobby and devising a manual modelling process shows noticeable independent thinking beyond a textbook example, yet the mathematical pathway (basic sine fitting) follows standard classroom techniques.  Evidence is therefore âsignificantâ but not outstanding.

Headings, sub-headings, tables and numbered figures demonstrate deliberate structure.  The student opens with an introduction and closes with a conclusion and reflection.  Minor formatting issues (e.g. duplicated figure labels, long uninterrupted text blocks) keep the work from the highest band, but organisation is clearly âwell-organised with clear structureâ.

Transforming sinusoidal functions and differentiation are fully within SL level and are applied correctly.  No content falls below level, but the work does not extend to higher-level topics, justifying the âfully commensurateâ band.

annotations.comment: The dual tables present the temperature data but lack clear headings and spacing; refine layout to avoid reader confusion.
References to Figures 3 and 4 appear before those figures are introduced; reorder or renumber for logical flow.
The scatter plot effectively displays the monthly data and is well integrated into the narrative.
The aim is generally clear but would benefit from a precise hypothesis and mention of expected model outcomes.
The title is engaging and clearly frames the mathematical focus on climate modeling, demonstrating strong coherence in presentation.
The student ID line contributes little to the mathematical narrative and could be removed to maintain conciseness.
Personal skiing experience gives the exploration authenticity and shows significant independent thinking.
The opening anecdote is lengthy and distracts from the mathematical focus; consider trimming to essentials.
The axis label uses the Greek letter Î½ instead of the more conventional lowercase v; standardize notation for clarity.
When introducing \(f(x)=\sin(x)\), explicitly state domain restrictions inline to reinforce mathematical communication.
The domain restriction statement lacks proper inequality punctuation and braces; ensure notation is precise.
The explanation of transformation variables is clear and shows effective use of mathematical language.
Figure 7 is mentioned without any contextual description in the text; add a caption or verbal linkage.
The standard period formula is applied correctly before solving for \(b\), demonstrating good mathematical understanding.
The paragraph explaining how \(b\) affects period is verbose; consider condensing to maintain conciseness.
The concept of period stretching is well articulated and enhances clarity of the transformation process.
Figure 10âs axes are not clearly labeled in the image; ensure each graph includes units and axis titles.
The notation for the vertical shift reference uses Î¸ inconsistently; use the same symbol for clarity.
Vertical translation calculation is clearly presented, demonstrating appropriate use of transformations.
Horizontal shift variable appears as âdâ and âcâ interchangeably; maintain consistent variable naming.
Adjusting the shift to address outliers shows thoughtful initiative to improve model fit.
The final sine function is clearly summarized with proper LaTeX notation, aiding mathematical communication.
The units in the outlier calculation use superscript formatting inconsistently; standardize Â°C formatting.
The evaluation of overestimation and underestimation demonstrates thoughtful critical analysis.
Reflection on model limitations is present but could link more explicitly to proposed future methodological improvements.
The final function for 2020 is succinctly stated and mirrors earlier structure, showing consistency in use of mathematics.
